The core task is to find the speed of the sailboat when the wind power is at its maximum. This requires analyzing the given formula and determining the conditions under which the power is maximized.

The first module identifies the core objective of the task, which is to calculate the speed of the sailboat when the wind power reaches its maximum value using the provided formula.

The second module analyzes the given formula to determine how the wind power depends on the speed of the sailboat and the wind speed. It also identifies the constraints, such as the values of $ B $, $ S $, $ \rho $, and $ v_0 $, and the relationship between the power and the speed.

The third module assigns the task to a specific aspect, ensuring that each module addresses a distinct part of the task. This includes defining the role of the formula, identifying the constraints, determining the output format, and ensuring that the solution adheres to the given conditions.

The fourth module ensures that all aspects of the task are addressed and that the final response meets all constraints, including the word count, starting/ending word, and keyword limits.
